food_subgroup: Instant noodle dishes with egg and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indomie Jumbo with egg and salad; closest match is the generic instant noodle food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GKM covers prepared instant noodles; no specific code exists for the Indomie Jumbo variant with egg and salad toppings.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific instant noodle dish variant.

main_ingredients: instant noodles (wheat flour, palm oil, seasoning)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, comprising a jumbo portion of cooked Indomie instant noodles, one sunny-side-up egg, and a small side salad of fresh vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Indomie Jumbo (With Egg & Salad) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Indomie Jumbo (With Egg & Salad), cr_id G00010435. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 18 g/100g based on the known palm-oil-fried noodle composition; trans fat is estimated at approximately 0.5 g/100g consistent with palm-oil-fried instant noodle products. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat breakdowns are not reported in the HPB record and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Instant noodles, white wheat flour — GI value 73 (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Indomie Jumbo with Egg and Salad. GI of 73 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white wheat flour instant noodles, which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (73) × carbohydrate per serving (20.7 g from 350 g × 5.9 g/100g) / 100 = 15.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; potassium at 64 mg/100g provides negligible counter-balance
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this dish or, at minimum, use only half the seasoning packet and avoid adding soy sauce or chili sauce, as sodium at 810 mg/100g substantially exceeds safe limits for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (73) refined wheat noodles combined with very high fat (44.5 g/100g, estimated saturated fat ~18 g/100g) worsens insulin resistance; zero dietary fibre removes any glycaemic buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this dish due to its high glycaemic index, negligible dietary fibre, and very high saturated fat content, all of which impair glycaemic control and worsen insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~18 g/100g, estimated trans fat ~0.5 g/100g, sodium 810 mg/100g, and cholesterol 26 mg/100g collectively pose a high cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is strongly unsuitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its very high saturated fat, estimated trans fat from palm-oil-fried noodles, and critically elevated sodium, all of which raise LDL cholesterol and blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated trans fat (~0.5 g/100g) from palm-oil-fried instant noodles is strongly pro-inflammatory; high saturated fat and sodium further worsen joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish as the trans fat from palm-oil-fried noodles and high saturated fat are strongly pro-inflammatory and will exacerbate joint inflammation and tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Indomie Jumbo with Egg and Salad is a popular hawker-style dish served widely in Singapore, consisting of a jumbo portion of Indomie brand instant noodles — Indonesia's best-known instant noodle product — accompanied by a sunny-side-up fried egg and a small side salad of fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, and cucumber. The noodles are made from refined wheat flour and are pre-fried in palm oil during manufacturing, giving them a characteristic rich, slightly oily flavour and a soft, springy texture when cooked. The seasoning packet, which contains salt, MSG, and flavour enhancers, is mixed into the cooked noodles, contributing the dish's signature savoury taste. Nutritionally, this dish is calorie-dense at 430 kcal/100g, driven primarily by its very high fat content of 44.5 g/100g, of which approximately 18 g/100g is estimated saturated fat and approximately 0.5 g/100g is estimated trans fat from the palm-oil-frying process. Carbohydrate content is relatively low at 5.9 g/100g for the composite dish, but the refined wheat noodles carry a high glycaemic index of approximately 73. Sodium is critically elevated at 810 mg/100g, making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, Type 2 Diabetes, or rheumatoid arthritis. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0 g/100g. The dish is a comfort food and a cultural staple in Singapore's hawker food scene, reflecting the widespread adoption of Indonesian instant noodle culture across Southeast Asia.

How many calories in Indomie Jumbo with egg and salad?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010435, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from known palm-oil-fried instant noodle composition and documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white wheat flour instant noodles.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the reported minerals is not available in the HPB Foodo database for this dish. Saturated fat estimated at ~18 g/100g and trans fat estimated at ~0.5 g/100g based on known composition of palm-oil-fried instant noodle products; these are not directly reported in the HPB source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. GI of 73 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white wheat flour instant noodles; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. This dish is classified as Indonesian cuisine but is widely consumed in Singapore as a hawker staple; multilingual naming follows Indonesian cuisine rules (Indonesian local name mandatory). Chinese simplified name provided as a supplementary alias for Singapore market context. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names added to satisfy G6 for Singaporean context, as this dish is widely consumed there.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type to 'closest_parent' from 'approximate' to align with allowed values.

Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to local_names and aliases_by_language, and to the flat aliases array, to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singaporean context (as the dish is widely consumed there).
Updated Q1 and Q8 notes to reflect the expanded multilingual coverage for the Singaporean context.
